audit_summary: Phase 3 audit confirmed Phase 2 food-label thresholds (400 mg sodium / 10 g sugar / 4 g sat fat per 100 g solid) remain current as of May 2026 with no Phase 3 announced. Added: label exemption list, Israel-specific nutrient gaps (iodine, vitamin D, iron, B12, folic acid), HMO dietitian entitlement (14 visits/year for diabetes, confirmed across all four HMOs), Wegovy/Ozempic GLP-1 policy update (Wegovy added to 2026 sal briut for adolescents 12-18 with severe obesity per February 2026 Health Basket Committee; adults remain out-of-pocket), celiac/Standard 1145 gluten-free notes, and Ramadan/Yom Kippur high-risk diabetic medication warning (insulin + sulfonylureas).

claims.claim: Phase 2 (January 2021) red-label thresholds: 400 mg sodium, 10 g total sugars, 4 g saturated fat per 100 g solid food.
claims.note: Confirmed via Health Ministry Efsharibari portal and FoodNavigator Israel coverage.

claims.claim: Liquid-food thresholds per 100 ml: 300 mg sodium, 5 g sugars, 3 g saturated fat (Phase 2).
claims.note: Multiple regulatory sources align on per-100ml liquid thresholds.

claims.claim: Front-of-package labeling exempts fresh produce, fresh meat/fish/eggs (single-ingredient), restaurant food, multipacks, packages under 25 cm² front area, and specific products (tea, coffee, yeast, food extracts).
claims.note: USDA FAS country report and FoodNavigator confirm exempted categories.

claims.claim: All four HMOs (Clalit, Maccabi, Meuhedet, Leumit) cover 14 dietitian visits/year for diabetes patients via the National Diabetes Program with a family-doctor referral; Maccabi does not require referral for diabetes, prediabetes, or gestational diabetes.
claims.note: Cross-referenced: Clalit (clalit.co.il dietitian-meeting page), Maccabi (maccabi4u.co.il eligibility), Leumit (leumit.co.il health-basket page confirms up to 14 dietitian visits/year for diabetes with quarterly copay), Meuhedet (meuhedet.co.il adult diabetes-rights page confirms 14 visits/year). 14-visit/year entitlement under National Diabetes Program is uniform across all four HMOs.

claims.claim: Wegovy (semaglutide) coverage splits by age: as of 2026 sal briut (Health Basket Committee decision, February 2026), adolescents 12-18 with severe obesity (BMI at or above 95th percentile, weight 60 kg or more, plus a related comorbidity) are covered (~4,500 teens eligible). Adults remain out-of-pocket at ~NIS 600-1,300/month; supplementary insurance covers about half for many policies.
claims.note: Haaretz coverage of February 2026 Health Basket Committee submission to Minister Katz documents Wegovy added for ages 12-18 with strict eligibility criteria (BMI percentile, 60 kg, comorbidity). Cross-referenced with Jerusalem Post article-874063 (2026 basket approval) and Times of Israel (April 2024 letter on Ozempic off-label end + adult Wegovy out-of-pocket pricing).

claims.claim: Since April 2024 Health Ministry letter, Ozempic may no longer be prescribed off-label for weight loss in Israel; patients with active Form 29 may continue until expiry then switch to Wegovy.
claims.note: Confirmed via Times of Israel reporting on Health Ministry letter to medical community dated April 10, 2024.

claims.claim: Israel has documented persistent iodine deficiency. National biomonitoring 2020-21 found median urinary iodine concentration 64.5 µg/L in adults, below WHO adequacy floor of 100 µg/L.
claims.note: Peer-reviewed biomonitoring study confirms persistent deficiency; salt iodization is voluntary and only ~5% of adults consume iodized salt.

claims.claim: Israel has no mandatory salt iodization policy; iodized salt is a small share of the retail market and sold at premium prices.
claims.note: Jerusalem Post confirms ongoing Knesset debate on mandating iodization.

claims.claim: Israeli Health Ministry recommends 800-1,000 IU/day vitamin D supplement for adults of normal weight with limited sun exposure; up to 2,000 IU/day for adults with obesity.
claims.note: Health Ministry supplementation guidance reported in TOI; covered as standing recommendation.

claims.claim: Israel has one of the highest per-capita vegan rates globally at approximately 5% of the population (plus 8-10% vegetarian).
claims.note: Cross-confirmed by Times of Israel/Shamayim and World Population Review country rankings.

claims.claim: Raw tahini (100 g) contains approximately 426 mg calcium and 9 mg iron.
claims.note: USDA-derived values; whole-seed tahini may contain higher iron (up to ~36 mg).

claims.claim: Diabetes prevalence in Israeli adults ages 20-79 reached 10.1% in 2024 (World Bank data); ~400,000 diagnosed diabetics; type 2 represents ~90% of cases.
claims.note: World Bank/IDF Atlas data; Arab Israeli prevalence (12%) significantly higher than Jewish Israeli (6.2%).

claims.claim: Celiac disease prevalence in Israel is approximately 1%, in line with global rates; gluten-free claims under Israeli Standard 1145 follow Codex Alimentarius threshold of <20 mg/kg gluten.
claims.note: Standard 1145 covers general prepackaged-food labeling; <20 mg/kg is the global Codex threshold also applied in Israel.

claims.claim: International diabetes-Ramadan guidelines (IDF/DAR) classify patients on insulin or sulfonylureas as high to very-high risk; pre-Ramadan medication adjustment, education, and weekly monitoring are required if fasting proceeds.
claims.note: Endotext clinical chapter on Diabetes Management During Ramadan; cross-referenced with 2021 IDF/DAR practical guidelines.

claims.claim: Nutritional Rainbow (Keshet Tazonit) is the official Israeli dietary-guideline framework, published 2019 and updated since; structured by consumption frequency rather than food groups; explicitly grounded in Mediterranean diet principles.
claims.note: Health Ministry Efsharibari portal is canonical source; current as of 2026.

claims.claim: Pre-conception and first-trimester folic acid supplementation (typically 400 µg/day, 5 mg/day in higher-risk women) is the standard recommendation to reduce neural-tube defects, prescribed at no extra cost via kupat cholim OB-GYN.
claims.note: Israeli MoH guideline studied in peer-reviewed work; standard public-health recommendation.

claims.claim: Saxenda (liraglutide) generic became available in Israel in August 2025 via Teva.
claims.note: Cross-referenced with pharmaceutical industry sources; Teva launched first liraglutide generic for weight loss August 2025.

claims.claim: Red meat recommendation: max 300 g/week home-cooked unprocessed red meat; processed meats (naknikiot, pastrami, frozen patties) are categorized 'below the rainbow' and minimized, not counted toward the 300g.
claims.note: Official Nutritional Rainbow guidance from Health Ministry.
